A `surprise` marks where your world model is wrong -- re-probe there instead of pressing on.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after clicking on the left blue square. Let me check what changed by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Nodes after click:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} children={n['children']}")

# Compare with previous frame
if last_transition:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    
    before_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}
    
    print("\nBefore hashes:", set(before_nodes.keys()))
    print("After hashes:", set(after_nodes.keys()))
    
    # Check for new or changed nodes
    for n in after_seg['nodes']:
        if n['hash'] not in before_nodes:
            print(f"\nNew node: id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
            print(f"  boundary: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The top and bottom borders changed. Before: both were black (B) with 64 pixels. After: they're now split into B(63) + W(1) each. The white pixels are at corners:
- Top right: [0, 63] is white
- Bottom left: [63, 0] is white
This seems like a timer or progress indicator. The black borders are "shrinking" and being replaced by white. This is likely a countdown/progress bar, not gameplay state.
The actual maze structure (yellow, orange, black maze, blue squares) hasn't changed. So clicking on the blue square didn't do anything meaningful to the puzzle itself - it just changed the border.
Let me try arrow keys to see if there's a player character that moves. Maybe the player is at some default position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The border changed (likely a timer/progress bar), but the maze didn't change.
# Let me try arrow keys to see if there's a movable player character.
# Try moving RIGHT first
predict(expect_change=True, note='Arrow keys might move a player character')
action(['RIGHT'])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

# Check segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nNodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
